title: Moody's Analytics Time Series
description: Schema for a Moody's Analytics Data Buffet time series response, representing economic, demographic, or financial observations and forecasts over a date range with associated metadata.

mnemonic.description: The unique series mnemonic identifier following the Data Buffet naming convention. Mnemonics encode the concept, geography, and source of the data.

description.description: Human-readable description of the time series including the indicator name, geographic scope, and data source.
examples: Consumer Price Index, All Urban Consumers, United States

frequency.description: The temporal frequency of the observations in the data array. Indicates how often data points are recorded or reported.

source.description: The original data source or statistical agency that produces the raw data.

geography.description: The geographic area the series covers. Can range from global aggregates to subnational regions.

geographyCode.description: Standardized geographic area code (ISO 3166 country code or FIPS state/county code).

units.description: The unit of measurement for the series values, describing what the numeric values represent.

transformation.description: The mathematical transformation applied to the original data, if any.

category.description: The broad economic or demographic category the series belongs to.

startDate.description: The earliest observation date in the returned data range.

endDate.description: The latest observation or forecast date in the returned data range.

vintage.description: The vintage date of the data, representing when this revision of the data was published. Enables tracking of historical data revisions.

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the series was last updated in the Data Buffet repository.

isForecast.description: Whether the series includes forecast values generated by Moody's Analytics proprietary macroeconomic models.

forecastStartDate.description: The date at which forecast data begins within the series. All observations before this date are historical actuals.

seasonalAdjustment.description: Whether and how the data has been seasonally adjusted.

data.description: Array of chronologically ordered date-value pairs representing the time series observations and/or forecasts. Each element corresponds to one period at the series frequency.

Observation.title: Time Series Observation
Observation.description: A single data point in the time series representing an observed historical value, a preliminary estimate, or a forecast for a specific date period.

date.description: The observation date in YYYY-MM-DD format. For frequencies coarser than daily, this represents the period start date (e.g., 2024-01-01 for Q1 2024 or January 2024).

value.description: The observed, estimated, or forecast value for this period. Null indicates missing or unavailable data.

status.description: Indicates whether this data point is a confirmed historical observation, a preliminary estimate subject to revision, or a model-generated forecast.
